_schema.purpose: Registry of city labor contracts (MOUs — Memoranda of Understanding) with employee unions. Labor costs are approximately two-thirds of the General Fund budget and the single largest driver of the structural deficit. Each MOU represents a multi-year commitment that the council votes to ratify. The terms reveal whether any member extracted efficiency provisions, productivity benchmarks, or compensation restraint before voting yes.

status_values.pending_review: Contract identified but not yet obtained or extracted
status_values.extracted: Key terms captured in source extract file
status_values.scored: Terms incorporated into union_deference scoring or incidents

fields.document_ref: Unique key; matches extract filename in sources/contracts/
fields.salary_increase: Total compensation increase over contract term (% or $)
fields.productivity_provisions: Any performance or efficiency requirements included
fields.key_signals: What the terms reveal about council leverage and priorities
fields.source_extract: Path to detailed extract file, if created

seiu_1021_2024.unit_description: General municipal workers — the largest bargaining unit
key_signals: SEIU 1021 represents the broadest category of city employees; their MOU terms set the pattern for other units
No council member has publicly requested productivity data or efficiency tradeoffs before ratifying a labor contract in the scorecard period
Police overtime has exceeded budget for three consecutive years per the April 2026 financial condition audit — the MOU terms and any overtime provisions are directly relevant
Council approved a new police staffing plan; the MOU governs compensation for those positions
